title: Cryptographic Perfect Hash Functions: A Theoretical Analysis on Space Efficiency and Algebraic Composition
description: We analyze a theoretical perfect hash function that has three desirable properties: (1) it is a cryptographic hash function; (2) its in-place encoding obtains the theoretical lower-bound for the expected space complexity; and (3) its in-place encoding is a random bit string with maximum entropy. The expected space complexity is log₂(e) - (1/r - 1)log₂(1/(1-r)) bits/element, achieving 1.44 bits/element for minimal perfect hash functions with load factor r=1.
